content.full_text:
.                                                              .

                                        CITY OF
                                    CHULA VISTA

                          OFFICE OF THE CITY ATTORNEY

                                                                  Mar 15  8 57 AM '90


                                                         May 8, 1990


Fair Political Practices Commission
P.O. Box 807
Sacramento, Ca.  95804

Re:  Request for Opinion re Conflict of Interest

Dear Commission:

The author is the City Attorney for the City of Chula Vista and
is requesting advice on behalf of Councilman Tim Nader, an
elected councilman of the City of Chula Vista.

FIRST QUESTION PRESENTED:  "Domino-Effect" Conflict of Interest

Can Councilman Nader cast a vote on matters affecting the
establishment of an auto mall (such as a proposed development
agreement, general plan amendment, site plan, use of eminent
domain, or some other exercise of the zoning or police power)
which auto mall is located several miles from his residence if
the establishment of the auto mall could reasonably be expected
to have the following "domino effect": Auto Dealer X who
presently occupies 3.97 acres ("Car Lot") of an 8.83 acre site
("Potential Commercial Development Site") would vacate the
potential Commercial Development Site. Based on various factors
discussed below, it is reasonable to conclude that the vacation
of the Potential Commercial Development Site by Auto Dealer would
have the potential, but not inevitable or predictable effect, of
inducing the redevelopment of the Potential Commercial
Development Site in a manner that could have an effect of greater
than $0.00 on Nader's lot.

MATERIAL FACTS

A street map of the City of Chula Vista is attached hereto as
Exhibit A and may be alternatively referred to herein as either
Exhibit A or "City Map". City Map is drawn to the scale
indicated on Exhibit A.




      276 FOURTH AVENUE/CHULA VISTA, CALIFORNIA 92010/(619) 691-5037

Fair Political Practices Commission
May 8, 1990
Page 2

Attached hereto as Exhibit B is a map of the Montgomery community
area of the City of Chula Vista, and will alternatively be
referred herein as either Exhibit B or "Montgomery Map".
Montgomery Map is drawn to the scale indicated on Exhibit B.

In 1989, all of the area within the green boundary line was
included in a redevelopment project survey area.

All of the areas that are shaded in either blue, gray with hatch
marks, blue with square hatch marks and brown with smaller square
hatch marks are being proposed for inclusion in a single
noncontiguous project area, herein referred to as the Southwest
Redevelopment Project Area ("SWRPA").

Councilman Tim Nader lives at 828 Cedar Avenue on a lot ("Nader's
Lot") that measures 75 feet by 100 feet and is marked on the
attached map with an X.

Councilman Nader desires to vote on future autopark matters and
on the inclusion of various parcels in the SWRPA.

As can be seen on Exhibit B, Nader's Lot abuts against the
Potential Commercial Development Site, the area marked for
inclusion in the Southwest Redevelopment Project Area.

This author is assuming that because Nader's Lot is within a 300
foot radius of the Potential Commercial Development Site, the
Commission would determine that under FPPC Regulation §18702.3
(A)(1), Councilman Nader would have a financial conflict of
interest with regard to a decision to include the Potential
Commercial Development Site into the SWRPA since the decision to
establish the Redevelopment Project Area would have a financial
impact on his abutting residential lot greater than $0.

The Potential Commercial Development Site is identified on
Exhibit B by red boundary lines. In addition to the Car Dealer
X's lot, there are at least four other property owners owing 6
other parcels within the Potential Commercial Development Site.

The City of Chula Vista is considering establishing an auto
park. Two potential sites are under consideration. These sites
are identified on Exhibit A as First Potential Auto Park Site and
Second Potential Auto Park Site, respectively. ("Auto Park
Sites", collectively).

The City of Chula Vista is presently prepared to exercise its
zoning power through either general plan amendments, zoning
amendments, development agreement and/or site plan review and
conditional use permit processes, and may be called upon to

CITY OF CHULA VISTA

Fair Political Practices Commission
May 8, 1990
Page 3

exercise its eminent domain power in order to establish in one or
the other of the two Auto Park Sites an auto park use.

Car Dealer X, located in the Potential Commercial Development
Site, has executed exclusive rights to negotiate with the City
for the purpose of relocating his car dealership into one or the
other of the two proposed Auto Park Sites if such sites are
eventually established by the City.

The factors which make it reasonable for one to conclude that the
Potential Commercial Development Site would be "ripe" for
redevelopment as a commercial use are: the availability of large
lot depth, its corner location, high vehicular traffic adjacent
thereto, and the underutilization of other parcels within the
Potential Commercial Development Site (thereby reducing the cost
of redeveloping).

The City has received indications of interest by at least two
commercial developers interested in developing the Potential
Commercial Development Site in the event the City is successful
in relocating Car Dealer X and acquiring his site along with the
other parcels in the Potential Commercial Development Site.
Except for these indications of interest, the City has entered
into no agreements including no exclusive rights to negotiate for
the development of the Potential Commercial Development Site.

Hence the question is presented for your consideration:

Can Councilman Nader cast a vote in connection with the City's
exercise of either the zoning or eminent domain power to further
the establishment of one or the other of the Auto Park Sites, the
argument being that a decision to establish an auto park would
result in the "freeing up" of the Potential Commercial
Development Site leading to the potential commercial development
of that site which, if it should occur, would have a financial
effect greater than $0 on Mr. Nader's Lot?

SECOND QUESTION PRESENTED: BIFURCATION OF INCLUSION OF DIFFERENT
PARTS OF THE PROPOSED SOUTHWEST REDEVELOPMENT PROJECT AREA

The issue will be presented to the City Council as to what
specific territory should be included in the proposed Southwest
Redevelopment Project Area.

Mr. Nader does not own property within the boundaries of the
proposed redevelopment project area and hence Health and Safety
Code §33130 would not apply to him. Even if it did, it would be
for residential purposes and no further construction would be
required.

CITY OF CHULA VISTA

Fair Political Practices Commission
May 8, 1990
Page 4


If the question of inclusion of a Potential Commercial
Development Site is bifurcated from the inclusion of all of the
other shaded areas on Exhibit B ("Other Areas") in such a manner
that the "Other Areas" are first considered for inclusion into
the project area, can Mr. Nader participate in the discussion of
their inclusion as long as the inclusion of the Potential
Commercial Development Site is not under consideration at the
time he expresses his opinion?

Background:

In addition to the preceding background presented in connection
with the First Question, the only additional information is that
the proposed Southwest Redevelopment Project Area, consisting of
all of the shaded areas on Exhibit B, is not dependent for its
economic success upon the inclusion of the Potential Commercial
Development Site, and in fact, there is no economic
interdependence on the inclusion of the Potential Commercial
Development Site and the other noncontiguous areas for inclusion
on the proposed Southwest Redevelopment Project Area. We have an
evidentiary basis for believing that the property that is within
2500 feet but beyond the 300 foot distance from Naders Lot would
not have a financial effect upon Nader's Lot greater than $10,00
in market value or $1,000 in fair rental value of Nader's Lot.

If the decision to bifurcate the question of parcel inclusion
into the project area is made by staff or councilpersons other
than Mr. Nader, can Mr. Nader participate and vote in the
deliberative processes associated with including such areas other
than the Potential Commercial Development Site into the project
area?

In requesting this advice letter, I would request the Commission
to consider the following aspect of public policy making although
I do not believe that it occurred in Chula Vista. If the staff
of a city is charged with making recommendations to the City
Council as to what parcels of land are to be included or exlcuded
from a proposed project area and the ability to bifurcate a
question is not allowed to occur in the manner that is suggested
in the second question presented herein, the staff, who are not
charged with public policy making, could effectively silence any
opposition to a proposed redevelopment project area by identi-
fying parcels for inclusion which are within a disqualifying
distance from a councilperson's property interest, thereby
quieting his voice by intentionally creating a conflict of
interest.




CITY OF CHULA VISTA

Fair Political Practices Commission
May 8, 1990
Page 5


Both of these issues are matters of immediate and urgent concern
to the City Council of the City of Chula Vista and its residents,
and request your advice on or sooner than the 21 day statutory
time period provided for in Government Code §83114.

Respectfully,

[signed: Keith M. Stubb, Assistant City]
Bruce M. Boogaard Attorney for
City Attorney

BMB:lgk
7166a
Enc.

cc: Tim Nader
    w/o enclosures




CITY OF CHULA VISTA
